elements.sent-2411-003: The tension field of the identity map from a statistical manifold to a Riemannian statistical manifold, which shares the same Riemannian metric, is the Tchevychev vector field multiplied by negative one.
elements.sent-2411-004: We derive a new class of statistical manifolds that satisfy the semi-equiaffine condition based on the statistical biharmonicity of the identity map.
elements.sent-2411-005: Furthermore, we determine the statistical structures of this class, when the pair of the manifold and the Riemannian metric is a simply connected complete Riemannian manifold of constant curvature.
elements.sent-2411-006: A statistical structure is a pair of a Riemannian metric and a torsion-free affine connection that satisfies the Codazzi equation, and a manifold with a statistical structure is called a statistical manifold.
elements.sent-2411-007: Although statistical manifolds have been widely studied since their appearance in information geometry in 1982\cite{Am , statistical manifolds have also appeared in Affine differential geometry.}
elements.sent-2411-008: If we choose an equiaffine transversal vector field for a locally strongly convex affine hypersurface in the real affine space, a statistical structure and the Tchevychev vector field are induced on the hypersurface.
elements.sent-2411-009: In centroaffine geometry, the shape operator of the hypersurface is determined by the Tchevychev vector field\cite{Liu1995TheCT .}
elements.sent-2411-010: In contrast, in equiaffine geometry, the transversal vector field is chosen such that the Tchevychev vector field vanishes\cite{NomizuSasaki .}
elements.sent-2411-011: Generally, statistical manifolds with vanishing Tchevychev vector fields are said to satisfy the equiaffine condition.
elements.sent-2411-012: Riemannian manifolds are the simplest statistical manifolds.
elements.sent-2411-013: In Riemannian geometry, the tension field is induced from the first variational formula of the energy functional for mappings between Riemannian manifolds, and mappings that have vanishing tension fields are called harmonic maps.
elements.sent-2411-014: For example, the identity map on a Riemannian manifold is a trivial harmonic map.

elements.sent-2411-017: The tension field can be defined for a mapping from a manifold equipped with a Riemannian metric and an affine connection, to a manifold equipped with an affine connection.
elements.sent-2411-018: On the other hand, a statistical biharmonic map must be defined between two statistical manifolds, which is a class of mapping induced by a variational problem\cite{FU .}
elements.sent-2411-019: In particular, a mapping between statistical manifolds with a vanishing tension field is a trivial statistical biharmonic map.
